title: Rawza Kibbeh
description: A fresh and adventurous Ethiopian dish featuring flour, pickle, and a raw-friendly twist.

ingredients.name: 2 cups all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: All-purpose flour is recommended for texture and crispiness.

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: To enhance the flavors without making it overly sour.

ingredients.name: Zest of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: To add a touch of brightness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Pinch of cayenne pepper or chili flakes (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a mild spicy kick, optional.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Stir well.

Gradually add lemon juice and zest into the flour mixture while continuously stirring to form a dough-like consistency. The amount of liquid added may vary depending on your desired texture; aim for a slightly sticky but workable consistency.
Add sesame seeds to the dough and stir until evenly distributed.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1-inch in diameter each. Ensure they are not too hard or crumbly as this will affect their raw-friendly nature.

Place the prepared kibbeh on a flat surface and cut them in half vertically using kitchen shears to create a pocket for filling. The cut halves should be placed back together, but ensure that one side remains open (for easy eating).
Prepare your filling if you're planning on adding one, such as pickles or other raw-friendly ingredients like sliced vegetables or herbs.
Fill the kibbeh pockets with your chosen raw-friendly fillings. Press down gently to help hold in the filling and ensure it's evenly distributed throughout the pocket.
Place filled kibbeh on a parchment-lined baking sheet and cover them with a damp cloth or plastic wrap to retain moisture during baking.

Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake for about 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown. Check the internal temperature of one rawza kibbeh to ensure it's fully cooked; avoid overcooking as this can affect its texture and raw-friendly nature.
Serve warm with a side of pickles, yogurt, or any other accompaniments you prefer.
